properties.name: Ottawa National Forest - West End dispersed-camping search zone
properties.rule: Ottawa says free dispersed camping is allowed along roads and trails open to OHVs on Ottawa National Forest land. Cross-country travel is prohibited, so use MVUM/OHV-open roads and posted restrictions.
properties.notes: General search zone only, not a parcel-accurate legal boundary. Best used as a visual reminder that this whole west-end Ottawa block is worth checking for legal roadside dispersed camping.

properties.name: Hiawatha National Forest - West Zone OHV dispersed-camping search zone
properties.rule: Hiawatha has 2,000+ miles of forest roads open for motorized use. Use the West Zone ORV map / MVUM to identify legal open-road access and do not assume every forest road is open.
properties.notes: General search zone around the Hovey / Corner / Island Lake cluster where OHV-camping indicators make nearby legal roadside overnights more likely. This is a broad guide area, not a surveyed forest boundary.

properties.name: Chequamegon-Nicolet - Silver Cliff / Lakewood / Wabeno dispersed-camping search zone
properties.rule: Chequamegon-Nicolet dispersed camping is limited to 21 days in 28, the next location must be at least 1 road mile away, and dispersed camping is prohibited within one-quarter mile of a developed recreation site.
properties.notes: General east-side CNNF search zone for roadside dispersed camping near Silver Cliff, Lakewood, and Wabeno. This is a broad guide area, not a parcel-accurate forest boundary.

properties.name: Michigan state-forest dispersed-camping search zone - Two Hearted / Blind Sucker / Kingston / Culhane block
properties.rule: Michigan state-forest dispersed camping is generally allowed more than 1 mile from a state forest campground, outside posted or excluded lands, with a posted registration card.
properties.notes: This shaded block is a high-priority search area for legal state-forest boondocking between named campgrounds. It is a planning guide area, not a legal parcel boundary.
